privacy_erasure_dependencies.required_contract: authenticated seal/privacy-scrub with exact absence and ACK semantics
privacy_erasure_dependencies.minimum_unblock: publish the pinned engine route and known-answer contract before cloud can run privacy_erasure

privacy_erasure_dependencies.required_contract: claim and deliver erased-tombstone scrub work through the production reconciliation path
privacy_erasure_dependencies.minimum_unblock: add the cloud scrub claim/delivery path after the authenticated engine scrub transport exists

privacy_erasure_dependencies.required_contract: deterministic controls for pre-intent, post-intent, socket-write, engine-commit, response, linkage, export, cancelling, cancelled-before-ACK, terminal-failed, promotion, finalization, and ACK-outage boundaries
privacy_erasure_dependencies.minimum_unblock: source-built engine boundary controls must expose each named commit boundary without sleeps or polling races

block_without_change.summary: A block_without_change writer must refuse to mutate the catalog while an IndexLifecycleLease guard is held, and may release its guard only after the engine acknowledges; it never changes catalog rows behind the lease.

privacy_transition.summary: A privacy_transition writer drives the customer soft-delete, hard-delete, and reaper scrub state machine: soft delete marks the row deleted and bumps the generation to fence future writes, hard delete purges dependents before the target, and the reaper scrubs the catalog target only after the hard delete lands.
